description:
To properly set the owner of /var/log/localmessages, run the command: 
$ sudo chown syslog /var/log/localmessages
or 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/localmessages
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/localmessages</tt> file contains log messages from certain boot scripts,
including the DHCP client, and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause: /var/log/localmessages does not have an owner of syslog|root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /var/log/localmessages, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/localmessages
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: syslog|root
title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log/localmessages File
